title: Key caches on lockfile hash with safe fallback
description: Build cache keys from a hash of the lockfile so they bust on dependency change, and use restore-keys for partial hits.
body: Use when adding actions/cache to speed up dependency installs or build outputs. Do not use a static cache key (it serves stale dependencies forever) and do not cache across untrusted boundaries where a fork could poison the cache. Actions: set key to a prefix plus hashFiles of the lockfile, add restore-keys for a degraded-but-valid partial restore, and scope the path to the real dependency directory. Validation signals include the cache missing and rebuilding when the lockfile changes, and a clean install matching the lockfile. Risk boundary: a cache shared with untrusted PR builds can inject code into trusted jobs; isolate or disable it and escalate if unsure.
